mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals weakening labor demand and future earnings pressure on corporations. A month-over-month contraction in PAYEMS predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as equity valuations compress in anticipation of lower corporate profits.
evidence_refs: fact:2011-08-15 PAYEMS at 131190k shows recent weakness in hiring
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with fragile labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where PAYEMS declines month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at near-zero levels (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) indicates maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, any upward movement in FEDFUNDS signals the beginning of a tightening cycle that reduces liquidity and increases equity discount rates, producing negative forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 0.25% from near-zero baseline
evidence_refs: fact:2011-07-01 FEDFUNDS at 0.07% indicates zero-bound monetary policy
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy with incipient tightening
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean return > 0% with t-test p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS rises from near-zero, or mechanism fails to activate in at least 3 separate tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly measure consumer and construction demand. When both series decline simultaneously over a 3-month window, it signals broad-based demand weakness that reduces corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 5% over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:2011-06-01 HOUST at 629k shows housing weakness, RSAFS at 390422M shows retail pressure
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.01 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) is a real-time measure of economic capacity utilization and manufacturing health. A sustained 2-month decline in INDPRO signals supply-side weakness and reduced corporate output capacity, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:2011-06-01 INDPRO at 93.07 shows post-crisis production levels
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following 2-month INDPRO declines are positive in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 3% year-over-year signals monetary policy pressure and real rate compression. In a low-rate environment, accelerating inflation erodes equity real returns and forces central bank tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market anticipates policy normalization.
evidence_refs: fact:2011-06-01 CPIAUCSL at 224.304 reflects post-crisis price levels
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean return > 0% with t-test p > 0.10) when CPI year-over-year exceeds 3%, or mechanism fails to activate in at least 2 separate inflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly measure labor market slack. When UNRATE rises while PAYEMS stagnates, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as corporate earnings expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp month-over-month while PAYEMS growth < 0.1%
evidence_refs: fact:2011-07-01 UNRATE at 9.1% with PAYEMS at 131190k shows labor market slack
regime_hint: post-crisis labor market recovery with persistent slack
failure_condition: Joint deterioration in UNRATE and PAYEMS fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of economic demand. Quarterly GDP growth below 2% annualized signals sub-trend expansion and reduced corporate profit growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized quarterly growth rate falls below 2%
evidence_refs: fact:2011-04-01 GDPC1 at 13270.1B shows post-crisis recovery trajectory
regime_hint: sub-trend economic expansion with growth deceleration risk
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean return > 0% with t-test p > 0.10) when quarterly GDP growth falls below 2%, or mechanism fails to activate in at least 2 separate sub-trend episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly measure productive capacity and labor utilization. When both decline simultaneously, it signals a broad-based supply shock reducing output capacity and employment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices stagflation risk.
candidate_trigger: both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:2011-06-01 INDPRO at 93.07 and 2011-07-01 PAYEMS at 131190k show supply-side weakness
failure_condition: Joint decline in INDPRO and PAYEMS fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.01 over any 18-month rolling window.
